KPI Bluebook
  • OEE %
  • View page source

OEE %

\[\text{}\]
Open in: VS Code PyCharm Edit
Description
  • Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) is a key performance indicator that measures the efficiency and effectiveness of manufacturing operations
  • It combines the metrics of availability
  • performance
  • and quality to provide a comprehensive view of equipment productivity
  • Overall Equipment Effectiveness combines Availability
  • Performance
  • and Quality into a single effectiveness score
Objective
  • The objective of OEE is to identify the percentage of manufacturing time that is truly productive
  • helping organizations to pinpoint areas for improvement and optimize their operations
  • Increase effective productive time by reducing downtime
  • speed losses
  • and quality losses
Formula

OEE is calculated by multiplying the availability, performance, and quality rates, which are expressed as percentages. This calculation provides a single percentage that reflects the overall effectiveness of the equipment.

$$ \mathit{Oee\,\%} = \mathit{(Availability} \times \mathit{Performance} \times \mathit{Quality)} \times \mathit{100} $$
Generation time 0.0s (AI 0.0s + Render 0.0s)
Total: 0.001s • AI: 0.001s • Render: 0.0s
Extraction rate % 100.0%
Error rate % 0.0%
Used in KPI(s)
  • OEE is commonly used in various KPIs related to manufacturing efficiency
  • production optimization
  • and operational performance assessment
Input Measure
  • The input measures for OEE include availability
  • performance
  • and quality rates
  • which are derived from operational data
  • Availability
  • performance
  • and quality factors calculated for the asset or line
Unit of Measure The unit of measure for OEE is expressed as a percentage.
Reporting Source OEE data is typically sourced from production monitoring systems, equipment logs, and quality control reports.
Comments A high OEE percentage indicates that equipment is being used effectively, while a low percentage suggests potential inefficiencies that need to be addressed.

Code Context

1-- KPI: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)

DEFINE MEASURE ‘KPI’[OEE %] = VAR Availability = 0.95 VAR Performance = 0.98 VAR Quality = 0.99 RETURN (Availability * Performance * Quality) * 100
